name: wifipumpkin3
title: wifipumpkin3

commands.name: captiveflask

arguments.description: show this help message and exit
arguments.raw: -h, --help            show this help message and exit
-t, --tamplate TEMPLATE

arguments.description: path of the static files from webpage
arguments.raw: -s, --static STATIC   path of the static files from webpage
-r, --redirect REDIRECT

arguments.description: The port for captive portal
arguments.raw: -p, --port PORT       The port for captive portal
-rU, --redirect-url REDIRECT_URL
-f, --force-login_successful-template FORCE_REDIRECT
-v, --version VERSION

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# captiveflask -h
 [*] CaptiveFlask v1.0.2 - subtool from wifipumpkin3
 usage: captiveflask [-h] [-t TEMPLATE] [-s STATIC] [-r REDIRECT] [-p PORT]
                   [-rU REDIRECT_URL] [-f FORCE_REDIRECT] [-v VERSION]
 
 CaptiveFlask - Server to create captive portal with flask doc:
 https://github.com/mh4x0f/captiveportals
 
 options:
 -h, --help            show this help message and exit
 -t, --tamplate TEMPLATE
                       path the theme login captive portal
 -s, --static STATIC   path of the static files from webpage
 -r, --redirect REDIRECT
                       IpAddress from gataway captive portal
 -p, --port PORT       The port for captive portal
 -rU, --redirect-url REDIRECT_URL
                       Url for redirect after user insert the credentials on
                       captive portal
 -f, --force-login_successful-template FORCE_REDIRECT
                       force redirect to login_successful.html template
 -v, --version VERSION
                       show version the tool

local_help.--help:
[*] CaptiveFlask v1.0.2 - subtool from wifipumpkin3
usage: captiveflask [-h] [-t TEMPLATE] [-s STATIC] [-r REDIRECT] [-p PORT]
                    [-rU REDIRECT_URL] [-f FORCE_REDIRECT] [-v VERSION]

CaptiveFlask - Server to create captive portal with flask doc:
https://github.com/mh4x0f/captiveportals

options:

  -t, --tamplate TEMPLATE
                        path the theme login captive portal
  -s, --static STATIC   path of the static files from webpage
  -r, --redirect REDIRECT
                        IpAddress from gataway captive portal
  -p, --port PORT       The port for captive portal
  -rU, --redirect-url REDIRECT_URL
                        Url for redirect after user insert the credentials on
                        captive portal
  -f, --force-login_successful-template FORCE_REDIRECT
                        force redirect to login_successful.html template
  -v, --version VERSION
                        show version the tool
local_help.-h:
[*] CaptiveFlask v1.0.2 - subtool from wifipumpkin3
usage: captiveflask [-h] [-t TEMPLATE] [-s STATIC] [-r REDIRECT] [-p PORT]
                    [-rU REDIRECT_URL] [-f FORCE_REDIRECT] [-v VERSION]

options:

commands.name: evilqr3

arguments.raw: -sa SERVER_ADDRESS -mu MATCH_USERAGENT -tp TOKEN_API [-d DEBUG]

-t, --tamplate TEMPLATE

-rU, --redirect-url REDIRECT_URL
-sa, --server-address SERVER_ADDRESS
-mu, --match-useragent MATCH_USERAGENT
-tp, --token-api TOKEN_API

arguments.description: Enable debug mode

-v, --version VERSION

commands.name: sslstrip3

arguments.metavar: filename - rite= filename Specify file to log to optional .
arguments.raw: -w <filename>, --write=<filename> Specify file to log to (optional).

arguments.description: Log only SSL POSTs. (default)
arguments.raw: -p , --post                       Log only SSL POSTs. (default)

arguments.description: Log all SSL traffic to and from server.
arguments.raw: -s , --ssl                        Log all SSL traffic to and from server.

arguments.description: Log all SSL and HTTP traffic to and from server.
arguments.raw: -a , --all                        Log all SSL and HTTP traffic to and from server.

arguments.description: Port to listen on (default 10000).
arguments.raw: -l <port>, --listen=<port>        Port to listen on (default 10000).

arguments.description: Substitute a lock favicon on secure requests.
arguments.raw: -f , --favicon                    Substitute a lock favicon on secure requests.

arguments.description: Kill sessions in progress.
arguments.raw: -k , --killsessions               Kill sessions in progress.

arguments.description: Enable response tampering with settings from <config>.
arguments.raw: -t <config>, --tamper <config>    Enable response tampering with settings from <config>.

arguments.description: Inject code into HTML pages using a text file.
arguments.raw: -i , --inject                     Inject code into HTML pages using a text file.

arguments.description: print(this help message.
arguments.raw: -h                                print(this help message.

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# sslstrip3 -h
 
 sslstrip 0.9 by Moxie Marlinspike (mh4x0f)
 Fork: https://github.com/mh4x0f/sslstrip3
 Usage: sslstrip <options>
 
 Options:
 -w <filename>, --write=<filename> Specify file to log to (optional).
 -p , --post                       Log only SSL POSTs. (default)
 -s , --ssl                        Log all SSL traffic to and from server.
 -a , --all                        Log all SSL and HTTP traffic to and from server.
 -l <port>, --listen=<port>        Port to listen on (default 10000).
 -f , --favicon                    Substitute a lock favicon on secure requests.
 -k , --killsessions               Kill sessions in progress.
 -t <config>, --tamper <config>    Enable response tampering with settings from <config>.
 -i , --inject                     Inject code into HTML pages using a text file.
 -h                                print(this help message.

local_help.--help:
sslstrip 0.9 by Moxie Marlinspike (mh4x0f)
Fork: https://github.com/mh4x0f/sslstrip3
Usage: sslstrip <options>

Options:
-w <filename>, --write=<filename> Specify file to log to (optional).
-p , --post                       Log only SSL POSTs. (default)
-s , --ssl                        Log all SSL traffic to and from server.
-a , --all                        Log all SSL and HTTP traffic to and from server.
-l <port>, --listen=<port>        Port to listen on (default 10000).
-f , --favicon                    Substitute a lock favicon on secure requests.
-k , --killsessions               Kill sessions in progress.
-t <config>, --tamper <config>    Enable response tampering with settings from <config>.
-i , --inject                     Inject code into HTML pages using a text file.
-h                                print(this help message.
local_help.-h:
sslstrip 0.9 by Moxie Marlinspike (mh4x0f)
Fork: https://github.com/mh4x0f/sslstrip3
Usage: sslstrip <options>

Options:
-w <filename>, --write=<filename> Specify file to log to (optional).
-p , --post                       Log only SSL POSTs. (default)
-s , --ssl                        Log all SSL traffic to and from server.
-a , --all                        Log all SSL and HTTP traffic to and from server.
-l <port>, --listen=<port>        Port to listen on (default 10000).
-f , --favicon                    Substitute a lock favicon on secure requests.
-k , --killsessions               Kill sessions in progress.
-t <config>, --tamper <config>    Enable response tampering with settings from <config>.
-i , --inject                     Inject code into HTML pages using a text file.
-h                                print(this help message.

commands.name: wifipumpkin3

arguments.description: show this help message and exit
arguments.raw: -h, --help            show this help message and exit

arguments.description: set interface for create AP
arguments.raw: -i INTERFACE          set interface for create AP

arguments.description: set interface for share internet to AP
arguments.raw: -iNet INTERFACE_NET   set interface for share internet to AP

arguments.description: set session for continue attack
arguments.raw: -s SESSION            set session for continue attack

arguments.description: interactive sessions can be scripted with .pulp file
arguments.raw: -p, --pulp PULP       interactive sessions can be scripted with .pulp file

arguments.description: interactive sessions can be string with ";" as the
arguments.raw: -x, --xpulp XPULP     interactive sessions can be string with ";" as the
-m, --wireless-mode WIRELESS_MODE

arguments.description: disable terminal colors and effects.
arguments.raw: --no-colors           disable terminal colors and effects.

arguments.description: Run the Wp3 RESTful API.
arguments.raw: --rest                Run the Wp3 RESTful API.

arguments.description: Port to run the Wp3 RESTful API on. default is 1337
arguments.raw: --restport RESTPORT   Port to run the Wp3 RESTful API on. default is 1337

arguments.description: Start the RESTful API with the specified username
arguments.raw: --username USERNAME   Start the RESTful API with the specified username

arguments.description: Start the RESTful API with the specified password
arguments.raw: --password PASSWORD   Start the RESTful API with the specified password
-iNM, --ignore-from-networkmanager IG_NETWORKMANAGER
-rNM, --remove-from-networkmanager RM_NETWORKMANAGER

arguments.description: show program's version number and exit
arguments.raw: -v, --version         show program's version number and exit

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# wifipumpkin3 -h
 usage: wifipumpkin3 [-h] [-i INTERFACE] [-iNet INTERFACE_NET] [-s SESSION]
                   [-p PULP] [-x XPULP] [-m WIRELESS_MODE] [--no-colors]
                   [--rest] [--restport RESTPORT] [--username USERNAME]
                   [--password PASSWORD] [-iNM IG_NETWORKMANAGER]
                   [-rNM RM_NETWORKMANAGER] [-v]
 
 wifipumpkin3 - Powerful framework for rogue access point attack. See:
 https://wifipumpkin3.github.io/docs/getting-started#usage
 
 options:
 -h, --help            show this help message and exit
 -i INTERFACE          set interface for create AP
 -iNet INTERFACE_NET   set interface for share internet to AP
 -s SESSION            set session for continue attack
 -p, --pulp PULP       interactive sessions can be scripted with .pulp file
 -x, --xpulp XPULP     interactive sessions can be string with ";" as the
                       separator
 -m, --wireless-mode WIRELESS_MODE
                       set wireless mode settings
 --no-colors           disable terminal colors and effects.
 --rest                Run the Wp3 RESTful API.
 --restport RESTPORT   Port to run the Wp3 RESTful API on. default is 1337
 --username USERNAME   Start the RESTful API with the specified username
                       instead of pulling from wp3.db
 --password PASSWORD   Start the RESTful API with the specified password
                       instead of pulling from wp3.db
 -iNM, --ignore-from-networkmanager IG_NETWORKMANAGER
                       set interface for ignore from Network-Manager
 -rNM, --remove-from-networkmanager RM_NETWORKMANAGER
                       remove interface from Network-Manager
 -v, --version         show program's version number and exit

local_help.--help:
usage: wifipumpkin3 [-h] [-i INTERFACE] [-iNet INTERFACE_NET] [-s SESSION]
                    [-p PULP] [-x XPULP] [-m WIRELESS_MODE] [--no-colors]
                    [--rest] [--restport RESTPORT] [--username USERNAME]
                    [--password PASSWORD] [-iNM IG_NETWORKMANAGER]
                    [-rNM RM_NETWORKMANAGER] [-v]

wifipumpkin3 - Powerful framework for rogue access point attack. See:
https://wifipumpkin3.github.io/docs/getting-started#usage

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  -i INTERFACE          set interface for create AP
  -iNet INTERFACE_NET   set interface for share internet to AP
  -s SESSION            set session for continue attack
  -p, --pulp PULP       interactive sessions can be scripted with .pulp file
  -x, --xpulp XPULP     interactive sessions can be string with ";" as the
                        separator
  -m, --wireless-mode WIRELESS_MODE
                        set wireless mode settings
  --no-colors           disable terminal colors and effects.
  --rest                Run the Wp3 RESTful API.
  --restport RESTPORT   Port to run the Wp3 RESTful API on. default is 1337
  --username USERNAME   Start the RESTful API with the specified username
                        instead of pulling from wp3.db
  --password PASSWORD   Start the RESTful API with the specified password
                        instead of pulling from wp3.db
  -iNM, --ignore-from-networkmanager IG_NETWORKMANAGER
                        set interface for ignore from Network-Manager
  -rNM, --remove-from-networkmanager RM_NETWORKMANAGER
                        remove interface from Network-Manager
  -v, --version         show program's version number and exit
local_help.-h:
usage: wifipumpkin3 [-h] [-i INTERFACE] [-iNet INTERFACE_NET] [-s SESSION]
                    [-p PULP] [-x XPULP] [-m WIRELESS_MODE] [--no-colors]
                    [--rest] [--restport RESTPORT] [--username USERNAME]
                    [--password PASSWORD] [-iNM IG_NETWORKMANAGER]
                    [-rNM RM_NETWORKMANAGER] [-v]

wifipumpkin3 - Powerful framework for rogue access point attack. See:
https://wifipumpkin3.github.io/docs/getting-started#usage

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  -i INTERFACE          set interface for create AP
  -iNet INTERFACE_NET   set interface for share internet to AP
  -s SESSION            set session for continue attack
  -p, --pulp PULP       interactive sessions can be scripted with .pulp file
  -x, --xpulp XPULP     interactive sessions can be string with ";" as the
                        separator
  -m, --wireless-mode WIRELESS_MODE
                        set wireless mode settings
  --no-colors           disable terminal colors and effects.
  --rest                Run the Wp3 RESTful API.
  --restport RESTPORT   Port to run the Wp3 RESTful API on. default is 1337
  --username USERNAME   Start the RESTful API with the specified username
                        instead of pulling from wp3.db
  --password PASSWORD   Start the RESTful API with the specified password
                        instead of pulling from wp3.db
  -iNM, --ignore-from-networkmanager IG_NETWORKMANAGER
                        set interface for ignore from Network-Manager
  -rNM, --remove-from-networkmanager RM_NETWORKMANAGER
                        remove interface from Network-Manager
  -v, --version         show program's version number and exit
local_help.help:
usage: wifipumpkin3 [-h] [-i INTERFACE] [-iNet INTERFACE_NET] [-s SESSION]
                    [-p PULP] [-x XPULP] [-m WIRELESS_MODE] [--no-colors]
                    [--rest] [--restport RESTPORT] [--username USERNAME]
                    [--password PASSWORD] [-iNM IG_NETWORKMANAGER]
                    [-rNM RM_NETWORKMANAGER] [-v]
wifipumpkin3: error: unrecognized arguments: help

  --password PASSWORD   Start the RESTful API with the specified password

  -iNM, --ignore-from-networkmanager IG_NETWORKMANAGER
                        set interface for ignore from Network-Manager
  -rNM, --remove-from-networkmanager RM_NETWORKMANAGER
                        remove interface from Network-Manager
  -v, --version         show program's version number and exit
local_help.-h:
usage: wp3 [-h] [-i INTERFACE] [-iNet INTERFACE_NET] [-s SESSION] [-p PULP]
           [-x XPULP] [-m WIRELESS_MODE] [--no-colors] [--rest]
           [--restport RESTPORT] [--username USERNAME] [--password PASSWORD]
           [-iNM IG_NETWORKMANAGER] [-rNM RM_NETWORKMANAGER] [-v]

wifipumpkin3 - Powerful framework for rogue access point attack. See:
https://wifipumpkin3.github.io/docs/getting-started#usage

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  -i INTERFACE          set interface for create AP
  -iNet INTERFACE_NET   set interface for share internet to AP
  -s SESSION            set session for continue attack
  -p, --pulp PULP       interactive sessions can be scripted with .pulp file
  -x, --xpulp XPULP     interactive sessions can be string with ";" as the
                        separator
  -m, --wireless-mode WIRELESS_MODE
                        set wireless mode settings
  --no-colors           disable terminal colors and effects.
  --rest                Run the Wp3 RESTful API.
  --restport RESTPORT   Port to run the Wp3 RESTful API on. default is 1337
  --username USERNAME   Start the RESTful API with the specified username
                        instead of pulling from wp3.db
  --password PASSWORD   Start the RESTful API with the specified password
                        instead of pulling from wp3.db
  -iNM, --ignore-from-networkmanager IG_NETWORKMANAGER
                        set interface for ignore from Network-Manager
  -rNM, --remove-from-networkmanager RM_NETWORKMANAGER
                        remove interface from Network-Manager
  -v, --version         show program's version number and exit
local_help.help:
usage: wp3 [-h] [-i INTERFACE] [-iNet INTERFACE_NET] [-s SESSION] [-p PULP]
           [-x XPULP] [-m WIRELESS_MODE] [--no-colors] [--rest]
           [--restport RESTPORT] [--username USERNAME] [--password PASSWORD]
           [-iNM IG_NETWORKMANAGER] [-rNM RM_NETWORKMANAGER] [-v]
wp3: error: unrecognized arguments: help

apt_metadata.depends: hostapd, iptables, iw, net-tools, wireless-tools, python3-aiofiles, python3-bs4, python3-dhcplib, python3-dnslib, python3-dnspython, python3-flask (>= 2.0), python3-flask-restful, python3-isc-dhcp-leases, python3-jwt, python3-loguru, python3-netifaces, python3-openssl, python3-ping3, python3-pyqt5, python3-pyqt5.sip, python3-requests, python3-scapy, python3-tabulate, python3-termcolor, python3-twisted, python3-urwid, python3:any
apt_metadata.description:
Powerful framework for rogue access point attack
This package contains a powerful framework for rogue access point attack,
written in Python, that allow and offer to security researchers, red teamers
and reverse engineers to mount a wireless network to conduct a
man-in-the-middle attack.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
